subject: Ecology
title: Forest Restoration
description: A micro-lesson that makes Forest Restoration usable.

hook.text: Planting a billion trees sounds heroic—but planting the wrong trees can make things worse.

buildup.text: Monoculture plantations lack biodiversity, store less carbon, and resist pests poorly.

discovery.text: Effective restoration uses native species, diverse mixes, and lets natural regeneration help.

twist.text: Sometimes the best restoration is simply stopping the damage and letting forests grow back.

climax.text: Costa Rica doubled its forest cover in 30 years—mostly by letting abandoned farmland regrow.

punchline.text: The best planting sometimes means stepping back.

quiz.question: Why can monoculture tree plantations be problematic?
options: They lack biodiversity and resist pests poorly
